observations.recommendation:
1.17 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. reconcile the accounting records with the property unit and determine the
causes of the variance for proper accounting thereof;
b. compute and recognize depreciation on all of the depreciable assets of the
Municipality;
c. prepare and maintain PPELCs with complete significant information; and
d. coordinate with the GSO-Designate and undergo disposition procedures
for non-existing/missing and unidentifiable PPE.
2. require the GSO Designate to prepare and maintain property cards for
reconciliation with the Accounting Unit; and
3. create an Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of all
properties as at year-end and henceforth, conduct an annual physical count of
PPE.

2.23 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
1. prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to revert the stale
checks to the Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account and
to recognize the appropriate payable/liability or equity accounts;
2. trace the difference of P231,734.41 between the reported balance per
books and adjusted balance per BRS and prepare appropriate
adjusting journal entries to correct the balance of the account;
3. investigate the validity of the reconciling items reported in the BRS
and accordingly prepare journal entries with complete
documentation to adjust the book and bank errors identified;
4. make representations with LBP Sogod Branch to verify the two
bank accounts that is present in the books but not confirmed by the
bank with balances totaling P211,562.21; and
5. henceforth, prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation
statements for all cash in bank accounts maintained by the LGU.
b. Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees one month before
the checks become stale. Stale checks shall be reported and attached to the
RCI prepared at the period of cancellation.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to communicate
with the source agency to determine the nature and terms of the dormant
account and cause its closure.

3.14 We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the balances of funds
received from NGAs with specific purpose to the Trust Fund account of the
Municipality.

3.15 We also recommended to the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Initiate the return of the unused balances of funds received from NGAs.
2. Trace the composition of the unsubstantiated balances to determine if claims
against the Municipality exist. If no claims exist, initiate the request for write-
off of the unsubstantiated balances of the Due to NGAs account.
3. Send confirmation letter to COMELEC and DA for the unused balances.
4. Initiate the return of unused balances of Priority Development Assistance
Fund (PDAF) and the unused balances from DOLE to the Bureau of
Treasury.

4.17 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1. prepare appropriate adjusting journal entries to:
a. recognize the completed projects in the appropriate PPE accounts duly
supported with documents; and
b. properly recognize to the appropriate Construction in Progress accounts
the accumulated construction costs incurred.
2. trace the causes of the negative balances in the Construction in Progress-
Infrastructure Assets account and investigate the variances noted in the
costs reported by the Municipal Engineer and per SL in order to
ascertain the correct balance of the CIP account;
3. prepare and maintain the CIPLC with complete significant information
on a per project basis;
4. provide adequate disclosures such as the amount and the status of the
projects under the Construction in Progress account in the Notes to
Financial Statements; and
5. henceforth, ensure that completed projects are adjusted to its
appropriate asset accounts.

5.22 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
• Direct the BAC to:
a) Execute the Contract Agreement only after the posting of the
Performance Security by the Contractor.
b) Thoroughly check for any negative slippage in the on-going
contracts and any misrepresentation in the bidding documents of
the bidder and disqualify the latter for any deficiencies noted that
warrants disqualification.
• Direct the Municipal Engineer to compute the liquidated damages for the
delay in the delivery of goods and completion of the project and impose
the same to the contractor.
• Approve only an extension of contract time if the request for contract time
extension provides the reasons or circumstances that warrant the
extension of contract time.

6.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Revenue Collection Clerks to remit to the Liquidating Officer daily and in
full amount the funds collected and the same shall likewise be
immediately remitted by the Liquidating Officer to the Municipal
Treasurer; and
b) Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections received including remittances
from the Liquidating Officer, intact and daily or not later than the next
banking day.

7.16 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Nurse/designated personnel by the MHO to:
1. prepare and maintain an accurate report on the Summary/List of
vaccines received, distributed and balances;
2. ensure proper monitoring on the movement of COVID-19 vaccines
thru reconciliation with Provincial Health Services Office (PHSO) as
to the correct number of vaccines received by the RHU based on
complete supporting documents; and
3. coordinate with the PHSO as to the scheduling of the retrieval of
expired vaccines from the RHU.
b. MHO Head to properly plan the utilization and distribution of COVID-
19 vaccines, including nearly expiring vaccines, to minimize or prevent
wastages thereof.

8.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Property and Supply Unit/MSWDO to:
1. prepare the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM for each
kind of relief goods to document the receipts, issuances and balances
of in-kind donations as a procedural control in the receipt and
utilization of the donated goods; and
2. prepare the Inventory List of Donated Items for DRRM based on the
updated Stock Cards and Property Cards;
b) MSWDO to prepare Acknowledgment Receipts for all donated items
received containing complete significant information such as the date,
control numbers, quantity, unit, article, description and value as required
in COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014; and
c) Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosures pertaining to
DRRM not evident on the face of the financial statements such as but not
limited to the quantity of received, issued and balance of donated relief
goods per category such as food items, medicines and medical supplies,
hygiene kits, and others pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated
April 15, 2014.

9.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a.) Require the HRMO to assess the existing Organizational Structure so that
the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established and submit a
report with recommendations thereon to the Local Chief Executive;
b.) Create clear-cut policies or written guidelines in the hiring JO personnel;
c.) Require Job Order personnel to submit primary personal identification,
undergo interview and the Immediate Supervisors to evaluate the
performance of each JO;
d.) Incorporate in the contract the duration of the work to be undertaken by
the JO personnel, specify the job description or a lump sum, piece of work
or intermittent job to be performed and require them to submit
accomplishments reports to support payments of wages; and
e.) Comply with the payment of wages to JOs based on the current daily
minimum wage rate as mandated by the Regional Tripartite Wages and
Productivity Board Wage Order No. RB VIII-22.

10.18 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Planning and Development Officer to prepare a
Revised/Supplemental AIP for new expenditure items to be charged
against the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF as approved by the
local sanggunian and Local Chief Executive (LCE) upon
recommendation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Council (LDRRMC) in accordance with the approved LDRRMP;
b) Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to:
1) ensure implementation of approved PPAs incorporated in the
LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) by identifying
programs and projects that are feasible and will improve the LGU’s
capability to respond to occurrence of disasters; and
2) refrain from appropriating and utilizing the LDRRMF for Projects,
Programs and Activities (PPAs) that is not within the four (4)
thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management.
c) Municipal Accountant to:
1) properly monitor the balance of the LDRRMF to ensure that the
yearly unexpended balance of the said fund is transferred to the
Special Trust Fund; and
2) process only those payments with complete documentation.

11.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC and the
Municipal Engineer to resolve the issues on the non-implementation of PPAs
and fast track its implementation.

11.14 Henceforth, the PPAs that shall be approved and appropriated are well
planned and implementation ready.

12.16 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a) prepare the annual Schedule of the Delinquent Stallholders and Lessees as at
year-end and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant; and
b) prepare Demand Letters indicating the total unpaid monthly rentals and
the penalties thereof to all delinquent stallholders.

12.17 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the books of
accounts the receivables on the rentals from the Market Stall and the
Government Lots, based on the report forwarded by the Municipal Treasurer
and the corresponding income earned therefrom.

13.10 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned
continuously adhere to the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020 to ensure the
maximum attainment of desired socio-economic development and
environmental management outcome of the Municipality is achieved and for
the realization of the intended purpose of the 20% Development Fund.

1.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1.8.1. Require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the
payees one month before the checks become stale. Stale checks shall
be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of
cancellation.
1.8.2. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary
adjusting entries for unrecorded interest earned of P3,493.60,
unrecorded checks issued totaling P125,142.05, unrecorded bank
credit memo totaling P223,350.00, book errors of P2.00, cancelled
checks amounting P101,702.30, and stale checks over 6 months but
not over 2 years amounting to P34,139.50.
1.8.3. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the schedule of
unreleased/unclaimed checks to the Municipal Accountant for the
preparation of the necessary JEV, to restore the amount equivalent to
the unclaimed checks to the CIB account and likewise to recognize the
proper payable/ liability account. 
1.8.4. Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the Cash in Bank, Local
Currency Savings Account for General Fund Totaling P2,117,550.00
to Cash in Bank, Local Currency Current Account and make
representation with the bank to verify the non-existing bank account
in the trust fund books amounting to P1,098,240.00
1.8.5. Require the Municipal Treasurer to make representation in the bank
to reactivate ten (10) dormant accounts maintained in LBP-Sogod
Branch.

2.16. We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:
2.16.1. Require the Inventory Team to complete the physical count of all
properties as at year-end and periodically conduct physical count of
PPEs which should be done annually;
2.16.2. Require the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer-Designate to
exert efforts to determine the composition of PPE adjustments
recorded in lump amount.
56
2.16.3. Require the Municipal Accountant to: (a) derecognize the PPEs
distributed to intended beneficiaries costing P10,109,418.49; (b)
coordinate with the Supply Officer-Designate and Municipal
Agriculturist to locate the missing PPEs costing P5,849,918.50 and
submit the acknowledgement receipt duly signed by the beneficiaries
as proof of receipt of distributed PPEs.
2.16.4. Direct the Supply Officer-Designate to institute measures to properly
dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties. Also, once proper
disposal has been undertaken, the Municipal Accountant should
derecognize in the books said unserviceable PPE items.
2.16.5. Require the Supply Officer-Designate to maintain property cards and
reconciled with the ledger cards of the Municipal Accounting Office.
2.16.6. Require the GSO/Supply Officer-Designate, Inventory Team and
Municipal Accountant to perform the One-Time Cleansing of PPE
accounts through the procedures prescribed in the COA Circular
2020-006.

3.19. We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:
3.19.1. Direct all Department Heads who are in-charge of the implementation
of projects/programs received, particularly the Municipal Engineer
and Municipal Agriculturist to implement the projects/programs
strictly in accordance with the work and financial plan timelines,
otherwise, a revised and approved disbursement schedule shall be
submitted for approval to the funding agencies;
3.19.2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to fast track the disbursement process
while ensuring complete and adequate supporting documents to
facilitate submission of liquidation and return to the funding agencies
all unexpended balance of fully implemented projects/programs.

3.20. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
3.20.1. Initiate the request for write-off of the dormant and unsubstantiated
balances of the Due to NGAs account pursuant to COA Circular
2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
62
3.20.2. Reconcile records with Comelec and Department of Agriculture for
any unliquidated fund transfers and resubmit fund utilization reports
if necessary.
3.20.3. Prepare a JEV to record correcting entries in both Trust Fund and
General Fund to correct the erroneous entries made for transfers of
partially completed infrastructure projects from Trust Fund to
General Fund.

4.9. We recommended that Municipal Mayor:
4.9.1. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the encashed checks
totaling to P3,109,931.49 and submit written justification on the
encashment of checks issued for fund transfer.
4.9.2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the issuance of checks in her
name unless the purpose of Disbursement is cash advances for
payroll, special cash advances and travel; replenishment of petty cash
fund; and reimbursement for travel expenses.
4.9.3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record in the books the
undeposited funds as receivable by reverting from the Government
Equity account the booked transfers of unspent current DRRM to TF
in the amount of P3,109,931.49.

5.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all the Revenue
Collection Clerks to remit to the Municipal Treasurer the full amount of
their collections on a daily basis regardless of the nature of collections made.

5.7. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit all her
collections, as well as collections turned over to her by the Revenue
Collecting Clerks, daily or not later than the next banking day.

6.16. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer
to:
a) ensure that all conditions preliminary to the commencement of
construction have been addressed and that a certification shall be
executed that detailed preliminary engineering has been conducted prior
to the start of procurement activities to ensure that necessary
requirements and documents have been undertaken for the project to be
completed as planned;
b) review thoroughly the request for time extension by contractors based on
complete and relevant documents that will enable the assessment of the
reasonableness of the reasons cited;
c) specify in the notice of suspension of work the period within which work
is suspended and the reason for granting the request to facilitate
monitoring;
d) ensure the timely issuance of notice of resumption of work, so that work is
started by the contractors immediately after the period of work
suspension; and
e) maintain complete and orderly files of documents pertaining to project
implementation such as approved contract time extensions, notices of
72
suspension of work and resumption of work to facilitate review and
monitoring, and to ensure that appropriate action are acted on time.

6.17. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
6.17.1. direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (a) to properly plan the
procurement of infrastructure projects; (b) to initiate the forfeiture of
Performance Security of erring contractors in favor of the
Municipality in case of non-completion of projects within the contract
period; and (c) to initiate the sanction of blacklisting erring
contractors, if warranted;
6.17.2. require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that
liquidated damages is imposed on every day of delay until the project is
completed and accepted by the officials concerned.

7.15. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor through the BAC:
7.15.1. To impose the liquidated damages for the delay of the delivery of
goods.
7.15.2. Submit justification why the BAC did not disqualify the bidders
whose line of business is inappropriate for the goods procured.
7.15.3. Submit justification why advance payment were provided to 888 ACY
Enterprises despite no written request from the supplier and no
irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a
commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond.

8.10. We recommended that the BAC submit justifications why the said contractor
was awarded with one (1) new contract despite misrepresenting himself by
not declaring all his on-going contracts. The justification is necessary for the
proper disposition in post - audit of the payments to the contractors, if to be
disallowed in audit.

8.11. We also recommended that the BAC scrutinize carefully the eligibility
documents submitted by interested bidders so that any misrepresentations
will be detected and to declare as eligible only those who strictly comply with
the provisions of the RIRR of RA 9184.

9.19. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
9.19.1. Prioritize the re-channeling of funds to other development projects if
the approved PPAs could no longer be implemented due to causes not
attributable to the fault or negligence of the Municipality and direct the Municipal
Engineer and MPDO to the fast-track and monitor its implementation and submit a
justification on the non-implementation of the four 20% DF projects in CY 2021.
9.19.2. Seek a separate SB appropriation ordinance/resolution, in case of lump sum
appropriation containing general terms, to serve as an authority to enter into
contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance with Section 22 of
RA 7160 and the applicable jurisprudence.
9.19.3. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that only projects aligned with
the purpose/objectives of the 20% Development Fund be funded from such
appropriation.
9.19.4. Ensure the refund of honoraria received by the frontliners during the conduct
Pfizer vaccination.

10.7. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
10.7.1. Compel the Municipal Budget Officer to charge disbursement from
the unexpended LDRRMF those that specifically support the risk
reduction and management activities of the LGU within the next five
82
(5) years and for programs, projects, and activities that address the
corona virus disease; and
10.7.2. Submit to the Audit Team a written justification within five (5)
calendar days why most disbursements of fuel out of the Special Trust
Fund – DRRM were not disaster and COVID-19 response related.

11.15. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
11.15.1. Rural Health Unit personnel and other concerned offices to submit
lacking supporting documents/ records/ orders to the Audit Team for
payment of SRA and AHDP and accordingly observe compliance on the
documentary requirements for these kinds of personnel incentives to avoid
suspension in audit;
11.15.2. Rural Health Unit personnel to submit a written justification to allow
payment of Hazard Pay and SRA on days that involved performance of
activities that does not directly cater to prevention, detection, isolation, and
treatment of COVID-19 towards the goal of reducing the number of cases
and death without prejudice on the issuance of Notice of Disallowance on
unwarranted claims.
11.15.3. Municipal Accountant to ensure (i) all necessary supporting documents
are attached to these claims before affixing her certification in the DV; and
11.15.4. Municipal Accountant to follow the proper recording of transactions to
achieve fair presentation in the financial statements, pursuant to IPSAS I.

12.9. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
12.9.1. Direct the MDRRMO, MHO-Designate, and other departments in-
charge of the isolation facility to submit the Attendance Sheet of
frontliners on duty and the List of quarantined PUIs, PUMs, and
Covid-19 patients duly certified by the Authorized Representative
with details on date of arrival and release from the facility.
12.9.2. Direct the MHO-Desigante to submit the complete list of attendance
during the conduct of vaccination program of the Municipality and
submit the distribution list of 280 t-shirts.
12.9.3. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to liquidate the cash advance for
various expenses and quick response and relief and recovery for
Typhoon Odette in the amount of P308,406.87 and submit the
liquidation report to COA for verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to secure snapshots of the bank statements from the depository banks of the Municipality and check the transactions in order to trace the negative balance.
observations.reason: There are still reconciling items which cannot be adjusted due to non-availability of previous years records thus difficult to reconcile. Exert effort to contact payee of those unposted checks to properly make adjustments to come up with accurate bank reconciliation statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to expedite the processing of land titles in favor of the Municipality. We recommended that no improvements be made on the land unless proof of ownership is established through a certificate of title.
observations.reason: Processing of land titles required fees and charges in which no budget has been allotted for year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the RCA be fenced with a secured gate at the entrance to minimize illegal visitors or scavengers. This will also help address occupational health and safety issues by limiting access.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to enforce immediate settlement of the audit disallowances and charges pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2017-021 dated November 3, 2017. Likewise, ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.reason: No finality of decision received thus having difficulty if deducted from payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit the required financial reports and comply with the regulation to submit monthly reports of their transactions not later than the 10th day of the ensuing month to the audit team.
observations.reason: Difficulty in meeting the deadline such as delayed release of checks due to non-availability of claimant, and sometimes for payment of supplier, and non-issuance of ORs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to review the CRAAF so that the correctness of the accountable forms in the custody of the Municipal Treasurer be ensured.
observations.reason: The CRAAF is not made available for review by the Accountant before submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict adherence to Sec. 4(6) of PD 1445 and check the completeness of claims as to the completeness of documentations before it shall be paid. Said measure must be carried out to prevent the suspension or disallowance during post-audit.
observations.reason: No reply had been received for the communication sent to PNP Chief due to his reassignment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE and come up with the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation on some properties of the Municipality; and reconcile its records against the General Services Office.
observations.reason: No enough time to reconcile due to the volume of work related to closing. Make sure the Municipal Inventory Committee to next year to reconcile accounting records with GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to review the transactions in order to trace the negative balance.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters for the liquidation of unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Accountable person was already out of service. Demand letters were sent to non active employee relative to outstanding cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Agriculture Officer to judiciously plan projects which are in consonance with the 20% DF guidelines.
observations.reason: Planning of projects was already made before this findings. They did not implement those projects that are not allowed per guidelines. They will make sure that for succeeding years planning of Projects will be all in consonance with the DF guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer that the provisions of the DBM-DILG JMC 2017-1 be strictly adhered to.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Focal Person to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant pertaining to available funds in the LDRRMF-Special Trust Fund so that the full amount could be programmed in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare liquidation reports for projects already completed in order to settle the obligation of the Municipality to the NGAs concerned; (b) direct the Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee to prioritize projects which are funded from fund transfer otherwise the unutilized funds shall be returned to the NGAs concerned.
observations.reason: Some projects are still on going as of year end. As soon as fund transfer is completed, liquidation report shall be submitted to concerned agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to itemize its estimated income based on the classification in the financial statement in the budgeting process in order to identify which type of revenue needs intensification in order to achieve the target.
observations.reason: There is standard forms required in the preparation of budget and financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management Officer to adhere strictly to Section 77 of RA 7160 in hiring job order workers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to train personnel who will man the VAW Help Desks in the barangays to capacitate them to address cases of violence against women in a gender-responsive manner.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management (SWM) Focal Person to consider the appropriate means of communication depending on the information that it wishes to pass on to the public and its stakeholders.

We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume and type of waste should be placed at the site, which will provide baseline data to assist in developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for waste management.

We recommended that a proper gate at the entrance of the RCA be built so that it is easier for the personnel at the site to stop vehicles coming inside; inspect the waste to confirm the type of waste to be disposed then direct the vehicle to the designated area depending on the type of waste already inspected.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management Focal Person to maintain disposal site records at the RCA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the allocation of funds to purchase the appropriate safety clothing as prescribed in the Manual for Operations and Maintenance of Sanitary Landfill; the Waste Management Unit should ensure that any person entering the RCA must wear the proper safety clothing so that health risks are minimized; The SWM Focal Person should ensure that the standards and processes prescribed in the manual are complied with to ensure that the operations at the RCA are effective and safe.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile the amounts withheld for each employee and remitted to GSIS in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of Due to GSIS, Due to Philhealth and Due to Pag-IBIG in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Non-availability of previous years records thus difficult to reconcile.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze the Due to BIR account and prepare necessary documents to support the remittance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make reconciliation of their records against that of the GSIS. Furthermore, the municipal accountant shall also verify who among the employees is deficient in paying its loan obligations so that an adjustment in his/her deductions shall be made.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant make reconciliation of cash in local treasury for CY 2018 and take-up the reconciling items as shown in the attached schedule and update the accounting records particularly the general ledgers in order to check the accuracy of the balance of cash in local treasury.
We recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant as well as the General Service Office to reconcile their respective PPE records.
observations.reason: The RPCPPE are still to be reconciled with accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the submission of the lacking documents mentioned in the detailed audit observations in order to be compliant with RA 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommend the BAC require the contractors to issue another warranty that is compliant to the provisions of IRR of RA 9184. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant must see to it that the correct amount of warranty security which is 30% of the total contract price be imposed if surety bond is used for the same future transaction.

Considering the LGU would continuously secure loans and borrowings to finance development and investment projects, the team recommended measures to address these deficiencies: - The maximum borrowing capacity should not consider the 20% debt service but also the LGU’s net available fund prior to contracting of loan/borrowings; - The LGU through the LCE should conduct self-assessment of their capacity to repay the loan taking into consideration their net available funds, ensure that utilization of loan proceeds is covered by appropriation ordinance, determine the most cost effective borrowing scheme, contract loan only upon proper evaluation of priority projects, and allocate sufficient funds to cover total contracted liabilities.
observations.reason: Reiterated in these years audit observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the constitution of an Inventory Committee composed of the Municipal Treasurer or/her representative and two (2) management officials that will be responsible in conducting the physical count and prepare Inventory Report of all Property, Plant and Equipment of the local government unit. The Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) shall be submitted to COA not later than January of each year. Updated Property Ledger cards (PLC) and Property cards shall be maintained by the municipal bookkeeper and treasurer.

Considering the LGU would continuously secure loans and borrowings to finance development and investment projects, the team recommended measures to address these deficiencies: - The maximum borrowing capacity should not consider the 20% debt service but also the LGU’s net available fund prior to contracting of loan/borrowings; - The LGU through the LCE should conduct self-assessment of their capacity to repay the loan taking into consideration their net available funds, ensure that utilization of loan proceeds is covered by appropriation ordinance, determine the most cost effective borrowing scheme, contract loan only upon proper evaluation of priority projects, and allocate sufficient funds to cover total contracted liabilities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate officials to conduct annual physical inventory which shall be reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) to be submitted to COA not later than January 31, of each year. Likewise, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each category of assets and the GSO-Designate to maintain Property Cards of all PPE by type to facilitate the reconciliation of records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor Designate the Municipal Accountant to record individual employee’s ledger contributions/deductions to support the balances in the subsidiary and general ledgers and regularly reconcile the amounts withheld for and remitted to Philhealth in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balance of Due to Philhealth in the financial statements pursuant to Sec. 111(2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor Designate the Municipal Accountant to maintain individual employee’s ledger for GSIS contributions/deductions and regularly reconcile the amounts withheld for and remitted to GSIS in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balance of Due to GSIS in the financial statements pursuant to Sec. 111(2) of PD 1445.

1.9 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant,
General Services Officer and Municipal Engineer to strictly follow the
accounting and reporting procedures pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008
and COA Circular No. 2016-004.

2.10 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct:
a.) the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare the BRS for the
reconciliation of the cash in bank balances and accounting records and
effect the necessary adjustments, where appropriate;
b.) the Municipal Treasurer to submit the disbursement vouchers and its
supporting documents to the accounting section as basis in the recording
in the books of accounts as deductions to Cash-In-Bank.
c.) the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the long outstanding checks and revert
back to the Cash in Bank account. If ever there would be claims on the
outstanding checks after being reverted back to the LCCA account,
require the claimant to request for a reissuance of the check duly
attached with the copy of the disbursement voucher.

3.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the General Services
Officer/Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit Report on the Physical Count
of PPE based on a complete physical count conducted and records maintained
at the Accounting Office in order to present a reliable and accurate balances in
the financial statements.

4.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Assessor to
expedite the processing of land titles in favor of the Municipality. We also
recommend that no improvements be made on the land unless proof of
ownership is established by a certificate of title.

5.9 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the General Services
Officer/Municipal Treasurer to undertake proper procedures to properly
dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 379 of
R.A. No. 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular
No. 89-296. Also, once proper disposal has been undertaken, the Municipal
Accountant should derecognize in the books said unserviceable PPE items.

6.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
a) require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the
expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the LCE
or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees
concerned to settle their cash advance.
b) cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and
employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period
pursuant to Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 after being sent
notice and demand. The Municipal Accountant is authorized to delete the name
of the official and/or employee from the subsequent payroll until such time that
the cash advance has been fully liquidated pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-004.
c) direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget
Officer to refrain from signing cash advance vouchers and obligation requests of
officials and employees with outstanding cash advances.

7.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor, being the head of the Municipal
Development Council to strictly require the preparation of the Annual
Investment Plan which should contain a detailed information of the specific
program, project and activities and which should partake the nature of
investment or capital expenditures.

7.9 The Municipal Accountant should likewise see to it that expenses charged
against the in accordance with prescribed guidelines.

8.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Council to maximize the utilization of the
MDRRMF in order to achieve the objectives of the fund and ensure
readiness of the LGU in times of calamity

9.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant:
a. to locate all the source documents and MOA with the source agencies to
review the terms and conditions corresponding to the funds transferred in
relation to the implementation of programs and projects;
b. to determine if the specific programs or projects for which the funds were
transferred were already implemented, goods/services were already
delivered, liquidation of funds were already submitted and received by the
source agencies, and liabilities were already settled for proper adjustments in
the books;
c. to return to the source agency any unused/unexpended balance if so
stipulated in the MOA;
d. to request authority for the write off of dormant fund transfer accounts in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if
warranted, duly supported by a quarterly aging of dormant fund transfers and
other relevant documents.

10.12 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to issue an Executive Order
creating the Oversight Committee in compliance to DILG- DOF JMC No.
2019-01 dated May 17, 2019, which will review the current Revenue Code of
the Municipality and recommend revisions thereon for submission to the
Sangguniang Bayan.

11.9 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to adhere to Section 77 of RA No.
7160 and Rule XI, Section 2 of the CSC MC No. 40 s. 1998, as amended.

12.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
formulate programs and projects and activities that address gender-related
issues and concerns geared towards gender equality and women
empowerment.

13.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor and the Local Sanggunian to:
a. craft ordinances and issue executive orders related to waste
management and environmental protection;
b. strengthen the current waste management system of the Municipality,
including, garbage classification, collection, and recycling;
c. add more waste recycling facilities; and
d. focus on the recycling methods of the recyclables.

14.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
to adapt monitoring mechanism to detect variances in the amount to be
remitted and withheld. Furthermore, we also recommended that accounting
records such as cash receipts journal, check disbursement journal, cash
disbursement journal, general and subsidiary ledgers must be updated
monthly in order to determine the exact amount to be remitted to the
PhilHealth.

15.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to adapt monitoring mechanism to detect variances in the amount
to be remitted and withheld. Furthermore, we also recommend that
accounting records such as cash receipts journal, check disbursement
journal, cash disbursement journal, general and subsidiary ledgers must be
updated monthly in order to determine the exact amount to be remitted to the
Bureau of Internal Revenue.

16.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to adapt monitoring mechanism to detect variances in the
amount to be remitted from the salaries withheld for the GSIS and Pag-IBIG
contributions of employees. Furthermore, we also recommended that
accounting records such as cash receipts journal, check disbursement
journal, cash disbursement journal, general and subsidiary ledgers must be
updated monthly in order to determine the exact amount to be remitted to the
GSIS and Pag-IBIG.

17.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exercise due diligence in
the processing of claims and complete documentations must be observed
before effecting payment.

18.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer
to promptly turn-over documents, reports and journals under their possession
to the Municipal Accountant on the prescribed time for onward submission to
the Audit Team within ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

We recommended that the Local Chief direct the Municipal Treasurer for the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 after being sent notice and demand.
observations.managementAction: Sent demand letters but still some did not comply

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief direct the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all possible remedies to recover the amount receivables from concerned farmers-borrowers.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments will be made this year

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief compel the Municipal Treasurer to render the accounts regarding check disbursements without Disbursement Vouchers.
observations.managementAction: Only 1 voucher was found and recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief compel the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere the practice of checking the completeness of documentation before claims are to be paid.
We recommended that the Local Chief direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the transfer of the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF to a Special Trust Fund to avoid misappropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments in the Cash in Bank and Cash in Local Treasury accounts so as not to overstate the Cash in Local Treasury amounts and correspondingly understate the Cash in Bank account.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented - adjustments will be made this year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the RSEFU for purposes of transparency and accountability in the utilization of the SEF.
We recommended that the Local Chief direct the Municipal Accountant to revert all dormant A/P accounts of the Trust Fund to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the fund transfers posted in the Inter-Agency Payable-Due to NGAs’ GL, maintain subsidiary ledgers and maintain a separate bank accounts for the trust funds that are being recorded in the same account.
We recommended that the Local Chief should consider creating and identification of Internal Audit Services, in line with R.A. No. 4177 and A.O. 70.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile regularly its records with the GSIS so that intended benefits due to the employee are not compromised upon their retirement from government service.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented - will consider creating and identification of Internal Audit Services, in line with R.A. No. 4177 and A.O. 70. The Municipal Treasurer will reconcile regularly its records with the GSIS

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE and come up
with the Report on Physical Count of PPE and attend training on Property and
Supply Management in-order to acquire knowledge on the conduct of physical
count of Property, Plant and Equipment;
 direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile its records against the records of
the General Services Officer; and provide depreciation on all properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 direct the Municipal Accountant to secure snapshots of the bank statements
from depository banks of the Municipality and come up with an accurate bank
reconciliation statements supported with paid checks, original copies of
debit/credit memos so that the validity of the reconciling items could be
established.
 direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Unit lacking
disbursement vouchers so that complete recording in the books of accounts
could be made and a reliable balances could be reflected in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
send demand letters for the liquidation of unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Engineer and Municipal Agriculture Officer to judiciously plan projects
which are in consonance with the 20% DF guidelines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget
Officer that provisions of the DBM-DILG JMC 2017-1 be strictly adhered to.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant
pertaining to available funds in the LDRRMF-Special Trust Fund so that the full
amount could be programmed in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare liquidation reports for projects already completed in order to settle the
obligation of the Municipality to the NGAs concerned; (b) direct the Municipal
Engineer and the Bids and Award Committee to prioritize projects funded from fund
transfers otherwise the unutilized funds shall be returned to the NGAs concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
itemize its estimated income based on the classification in the financial
statement in the budgeting process in order to identify which type of revenue
needs collection intensification in order to achieve the target.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource
Management Officer to adhere strictly to Section 77 of RA 7160 in hiring job
order workers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social and
Welfare Development Officer to train personnel who will man the VAW Help
Desks in the barangays to capacitate them to address cases of violence against
women in a gender-responsive manner.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.

14.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to
consider the appropriate means of communication depending on the
information that it wishes to pass on to the public and its stakeholders. For
instance, there should be public notices on television and on the radio about
who can access the landfill, the working hours at the Residual Containment
Area (RCA) to limit after-hours access and the schedule for collection of
wastes from households for the different zones.

14.12 Websites have become increasingly effective and economical in disseminating
information. Therefore it is important to have a specific link for Solid Waste
Management within the Municipality’s website so that it is easier for the
public to have access to information and policy on solid waste management as
well as any issue concerning solid waste management. To ensure effective
implementation of the solid waste management plan, a well-designed, current
and targeted community awareness program is required. The targeted
audience should be considered when determining how the information should
be given to the public. The urgency of the matter should also be considered so
that everyone is aware of the matter as soon as possible.

15.5 We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume and type of waste
should be placed at the site, which will provide baseline data to assist in
developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for waste
management.

16.7 We recommended that a proper gate at the entrance to the RCA be built so that
it is easier for the personnel at the area to stop vehicles coming inside; inspect
the waste to confirm the type of waste to be disposed then direct the vehicle to
the designated area depending on the type of waste already inspected. This
daily process will compensate for the non-segregation of waste at source and
will ensure that wastes being disposed at the landfill are properly separated.

16.8 We further recommended that the SWM Focal Person should implement a color
coding or appropriate means of identification to ensure that wastes are properly
segregated at the landfill.

17.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to
maintain disposal site records at the RCA.

18.5 We recommended that the RCA be fenced with a secured gate at the entrance to
minimize illegal visitors or scavengers. This will also help address the
occupational health and safety issues by limiting access.

20.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the allocation of funds to
purchase the appropriate safety clothing as prescribed in the Manual for
Operation and Maintenance at Sanitary Landfill; direct the SWM Focal Person
to ensure that any person entering the RCA must wear the proper safety clothing
so that health risks are minimized and ensure that the standards and processes
prescribed in the manual are complied with to ensure that the operations at the
site are safe.

20.14 We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to enforce immediate settlement of the audit
disallowances and charges pursuant to COA Resolution No 2017-021 dated
November 3, 2017. Likewise, ensure that all employees who are retiring or
transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges
for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No.
2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

21.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
regularly reconcile the amounts withheld for each employee and remitted to
GSIS in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances of Due to
GSIS, Due to Philhealth and Due to PAG-IBIG in the financial statements.

22.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
analyze the Due to BIR account and prepare necessary documents to support the
remittance.

23.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
make reconciliation of their records with that of the GSIS. Furthermore, the
municipal accountant shall also verify who among the employees is deficient in
paying its loan obligations so that an adjustment in his/her deductions shall be
made.

24.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to submit the required financial reports and comply with
the regulation to submit monthly reports of their transactions not later than the
10th day of the ensuing month to the audit team.

25.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to make reconciliation of cash in local treasury for CY 2018 and take-up the
reconciling items as shown in the attached schedule and update the accounting
records particularly the general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers in order to check
the accuracy of the balance of cash in local treasury.

26.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to review the CRAAF so that the correctness of the accountable forms in the
custody of the Municipal Treasurer be ensured.
We recommended that the Local Chief will conduct the physical inventory of all existing PPEs and compel the Municipal Accountant to prepare the physical inventory report for submission to COA.
observations.managementAction: Conducted physical inventory but some properties were not included resulting to incomplete physical count

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to explain in writing why two funding sources were utilized for the project.
observations.managementAction: The agency made sure that every projects come from one fund source only

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief direct the DRRM-C to strictly comply with the provision of Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: We made sure to follow the provisions of Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the Annual Budget an appropriation equivalent to 1% of IRA and cause the creation of the Local Council for the Protection of Children.
We recommended that the Local Chief compel the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9003 especially in the utilization of the SWM Fund.
observations.managementAction: We made sure to follow the provisions of R.A. 9003 especially in the utilization of the SWM Fund

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the transfer of fund from GF to BUB fund and make the necessary adjustment to present the correct cash account balances of both funds as of December 31, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The said fund was transferred to TF account

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief compel the Treasurer to make the necessary posting in the cash book to reflect the correct amount of cash balance in local treasury.
observations.managementAction: The said amount was deposited intact.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief direct the GAD council together with the LPDO to come-up with the database in attuning to the demands of GAD issues and concerns.
We recommended that the Local Chief in Executive compel the Municipal Treasurer to use and maintain a cash book for all the cash in bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: The treasurer has maintained cashbook in bank electronic copy but not all fund accounts

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant open and maintain a separate trust account for the PFPR fund.
observations.managementAction: Separate fund account were opened and maintained

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately reconcile the over-remitted amount due to the PAG-Ibig and compel the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile the amounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the creation of a
committee who will conduct the physical count of all PPEs of the LGU and
compel the Municipal Accountant to prepare the physical inventory report for
submission to COA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the
expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of
the LCE or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and
employees concerned to settle their cash advance.
b) Cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and
employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed
period pursuant to Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 after
being sent notice and demand. The Municipal Accountant is authorized to
delete the name of the official and/or employee from the subsequent payroll
until such time that the cash advance has been fully liquidated pursuant to
COA Circular No. 96-004.
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Budget Officer to refrain from signing cash advance vouchers and
obligation requests of officials and employees with outstanding cash
advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to exhaust all possible remedies to collect from concerned farmers
the repayment of their loans. This could be initiated by the sending of letters to
defaulting farmers requesting for repayment of their outstanding amortizations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal
Budget Officer (MBO) to comply strictly the provisions of Section 85 of
P.D. 1445 and in relation to the provision of Section 65 (a) (4) of Article
XXI of R.A. 9184;
b) Require the BAC and MBO to explain in writing why such transaction
should not be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Treasurer to render the accounts regarding check disbursements without
Disbursement Vouchers immediately in order to present an accurate Cash in
Bank account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Accountant to strictly adhere to the practice of checking the completeness of
documentations before claims are to be paid. Said measure must be carried out
to avoid suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit.
Likewise, suppliers of goods and services must be required to submit required
documentations as mandated under Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001
to ensure that procured items are from legitimate sources.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to effect the transfer of the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF to a
Special Trust Fund to avoid misappropriation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a.) Direct the LDRRMC to strictly comply with the provision of Section 3.0
of COA Circular No. 2012-002, thus, incur expenses within the LDRRM
investment plan; and,
b.) Compel the Municipal Accountant to promptly and regularly prepare the
required Report on Sources and Utilization and submit to COA for audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Budget Officer to include in the Annual Budget an appropriation equivalent to
1% of IRA for LCPC to adhere to the provisions of Section 15 of RA 9344 in
order to fund LCPC activities. Furthermore, the Local Chief Executive should
cause the creation of the Local Council for the Protection of Children and direct
the said council to prepare Annual Work and Financial Plan for the attainment
of the LCPC projects and activities for the welfare of the children.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Accountant to adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9003 especially in the utilization
of the SWM Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to effect the transfer of fund from GF to BUB fund and make the
necessary adjustment to present the correct cash account balances of both funds
as of December 31, 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the Cash in Bank and Cash in
Local Treasury accounts so as not to overstate the Cash in Treasury amounts
and correspondingly understate the Cash in Bank account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the RSEFU for purposes of transparency and
accountability in the utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Treasurer to make the necessary posting in the cash book to reflect the correct
amount of cash balance in local treasury and install stern internal control
measures in the recording of cash in consonance with Section F of the LTOM.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to make a Journal Entry Voucher reverting all dormant A/P
accounts of the Trust Fund to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to reclassify the fund transfers posted in the Inter-Agency Payable-
Due to NGAs’ GL, maintain subsidiary ledgers and maintain a separate bank
accounts for the trust funds that are being recorded in the same account so as
not to expose the fund transfers to misuse while not properly accounted for.

The Local Chief Executive should consider creating and organizing an
Internal Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. No. 4177 and A.O. 70 to give
assurance for a sound and adequate internal control system in the operations of
the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD
council together with the LPDO to come-up with the database in attuning to
the demands of GAD issues and concerns.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to use and maintain a cash book for all the cash in bank accounts in
order to keep track of all deposits as well as disbursements by checks made by
the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Treasurer to open and maintain a separate trust account for the PFPR fund and
require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate set of books for the
said fund, immediately.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Require the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA
8291 so that intended benefits due to the employee are not compromised
upon their retirement from government service.
2. Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be remitted
to GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month the
withholding was made or the withholding was accrued.
3. Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct
thorough reconciliation on affected employees with negative balances for
over six months to five years, and request from GSIS for refund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately reconcile the over-remitted amount due to the PAG-
Ibig and compel the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile the amounts
withheld and remitted to Pag-ibig in order to ascertain the accuracy and
reliability of the balances of Due to Pag-ibig in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to:
a. conduct a complete physical count of Property Plant and
Equipment;
b. submit to the Auditor the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) duly reconciled with the accounting
records;
c. list all Property, Plant and Equipment found on hand, classifying them
into serviceable or unserviceable;
d. maintain updated property cards per category on all property plant and
equipment as recorded in the list of Property, Plant and Equipment of
accounting records.
e. reconcile property records ensuring that the balances shown in the
prepared RCPPE agree with the supply records and the book balances
in the accounting records, and vice versa;
f. renew and maintain all acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE)
for the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared
RCPPE;
g. prepare the necessary Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property and Waste Materials Report for the unserviceable properties
found on hand, taking into consideration EO No. 888 March 1983,
COA Memorandum No. 2005-027.
2. Direct the Municipal Assessor to produce a listing of all the properties
owned by the government and reconcile them with those properties
counted by the committee with an explanation for those items that are
recorded in the books but could not be physically located;
3. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to determine
the validity of the PPE account through reconciliation of the accounting
records and the actual inventory;
4. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) for
each class of property, plant and equipment listing therein the data as
to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of
depreciation, disposal, and other information about the property, plant
and equipment, based on the source documents of the transactions;
b. Drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties after the
Waste Material Report and the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property are duly approved and evaluated by the COA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards
Committee, the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer and Budget Officer to strictly
follow the submission of documents and refrain from paying the said transactions if
deficiencies and/or incomplete documentations were discovered in accordance with
Section 12.2 of IRR of 9184 and Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Focal Person of
the LDRRMF and other local officials concerned to effect implementation of the
programs, projects and activities identified and programmed during the year out of
the LDRRMF to ensure readiness of the LGU in case of calamity.

We also recommended for the utilization of the unexpended balance for
disaster risk reduction and management activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC, Technical
Working Group and other persons involved in the procurement process to observe
and comply the provisions the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, where:
a. Procurement should be properly planned and that procurement of goods
and services, supplies, and construction materials must be done generally
through public bidding, otherwise, if they resorted to other modes of
procurement, the requirement mentioned in the IRR of RA 9184 on
alternative modes of procurement must be complied.
b. If the original mode of procurement recommended in the APP was public
bidding but cannot be pursued, the BAC, through a resolution, shall justify
and recommend the change in the mode of procurement, provided, the
requirements mentioned in the IRR of RA 9184 must be met.
c. Henceforth, claims must be supported with complete documentation to
avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the strict observance
of the provisions of Section 272 of the LGC, Article 363 of its Implementing Rules
and Regulations and Item 4 of Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 of the DECS, DBM and
DILG, in the utilization of the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Planning Development Officer, Municipal Engineer and other officials of the
municipality to implement the 20% development projects in accordance with the
mandate of the Local Government Code and DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2011-o1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and
Award Committee to strictly follow the requirements on the submission of the
perfected contracts and other supporting documents within five (5) days from the
execution thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MENRO, all the
Punong Barangays, and all other concerned for the adoption of a decentralized solid
waste management system by providing financial and technical assistance for the
establishment and operation of functional and standard-based MRFs in the barangays.
We likewise recommended for a wide dissemination of information on source
reduction, recycling and composting programs in conformance to the provisions of
the IRR of RA 9003.
